data.title: Sonova Holding AG reports 1H results
data.description:
• Sonova Holding AG press release (SONVF): 1H sales reached CHF 1,815.4 million.
  • Normalized EBITA rose by 16.0%, reaching CHF 316.1 million.
  • The company confirmed the outlook for the 2025/26 financial year and continues to expect consolidated sales to increase by 5-9% and normalized EBITA to grow by 14-18% in the 2025/26 financial year.

data.title: Trade tensions shift China’s export engine toward Asia in 2025
data.description:
Tariffs and escalating trade tensions between Washington and Beijing have remained a dominant theme throughout 2025, reshaping global commerce in unexpected ways. 

According to Apollo's chief economist Torsten Slok, China’s export landscape has undergone a meaningful realignment as companies adapt to shifting geopolitical and economic pressures. 

While U.S.-bound shipments from China have weakened this year, the decline has not resulted in an overall pullback in Chinese manufacturing activity. Instead, exporters have redirected goods to faster-growing regional markets. Apollo’s analysis highlights that Asia has become the primary beneficiary of this shift, with Chinese exports to neighboring economies rising sharply in 2025. 

Since January, the value of Chinese exports to the United States has fallen by roughly $75 billion—a reflection of steeper tariffs, supply-chain diversification efforts by U.S. firms, and heightened regulatory scrutiny. Over the same period, Chinese exports to Asia have surged by approximately $150 billion, helping offset the loss of U.S. demand and reinforcing Asia’s importance as a commercial hub for Chinese producers. 

Meanwhile, shipments to Europe, Africa, and Latin America have remained largely unchanged, suggesting that China’s export strategy this year has been less about broad global expansion and more about concentrating trade flows within its own region. 

See the chart below provided by Apollo:

S&P 500 ETFs: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (FXAIX), (VFIAX), (VFFSX), and (SWPPX).

China ETFs: (KWEB), (PGJ), (CQQQ), (FXI), (GXC), (MCHI), (FLCH), (CNYA), (ASHR), and (YINN).

data.title: DoorDash's Q3 sell-off creates buying opportunity ahead of a 'banner year' -- Needham
data.description:
DoorDash (DASH) revealed its aggressive investment plans for FY26 in robotics, fulfillment services, and Deliveroo, spooking investors and sending shares spiraling lower despite solid third quarter results.

Needham’s Bernie McTernan sees this as an attractive buying opportunity to capitalize on a 16% drop in shares at a time when 2025 is set to be a “banner year” for DoorDash (DASH).

“Given the context of DASH’s success as a public company, including on track to double their post-COVID gross order value ((GOV)) in roughly 4 years, we are surprised to the magnitude of the negative reaction in shares, losing almost $30B of market cap for an announced several hundred million dollar investment in future growth,” McTernan says.  

McTernan estimates DASH can grow adjusted EBITDA by more than 30% per year with organic sales growth and the addition of Deliveroo more than offsetting the increased investment in FY26. While this increased investment won’t lead to accelerating growth, they are necessary for DASH to keep their current growth trajectory intact. 

“While the headline dollar amount of these investments is large, and the incremental return does not appear particularly tangible at the moment, we think the current management team should be given the benefit of the doubt given the success they have shown over the past few years,” McTernan adds.

Needham reiterates its Buy rating on DoorDash but trims the target price by 8.3% to reflect the share price decline.

DoorDash (DASH) shares are higher Friday snapping a four-day losing streak and putting the stock in the plus column for the week for the first time in six weeks.

data.title: Scholar Rock outlines 2026 apitegromab launch plan and accelerates supply chain redundancy amid regulatory review
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Scholar Rock Holding Corporation (SRRK) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO David Hallal described the company’s transition following his appointment in April, highlighting confidence in Scholar Rock’s potential as a "global biotech powerhouse" with apitegromab in SMA and an expanding focus on other neuromuscular disorders. Hallal emphasized, “we know that it is not a matter of if but when apitegromab will be approved in the U.S. for patients living with SMA.” He clarified the September 22 Complete Response Letter (CRL) was solely due to the status of the third-party fill/finish facility, not efficacy or safety concerns.
  • Hallal provided a regulatory update, stating there was a constructive Type A meeting with the FDA, with Novo Nordisk affirming remediation at the Bloomington facility and expecting readiness for reinspection by year-end. Scholar Rock is preparing to resubmit the BLA and anticipates a U.S. launch of apitegromab in 2026.
  • The company is accelerating supply chain redundancy, having secured commercial capacity at a second U.S. fill/finish facility and commencing tech transfer, with an sBLA planned for later in 2026.
  • President of R&D Akshay Vaishnaw noted the urgency in bringing apitegromab to market, sharing that the sole approvability issue in the CRL was the manufacturing facility’s compliance. He confirmed continued collaboration with Novo Nordisk and progress on the European marketing authorization, expecting an EMA decision mid-next year. Vaishnaw announced the initiation of the Phase II OPAL trial in infants and toddlers and plans to begin clinical development of apitegromab in a second neuromuscular disorder by year-end.
  • Chief Operating Officer Keith Woods stated, "The SMA community is demanding more. Even with currently available treatments, they need a treatment that directly addresses progressive muscle wasting." He discussed expanded engagement with SMA treatment centers and payers, and readiness for rapid commercial execution upon approval.
  • CFO Vikas Sinha reported, "We ended the third quarter with $369.6 million in cash and cash equivalents" and continued disciplined financial operations, deferring some investments and focusing on six prioritized initiatives including the acceleration of supply chain redundancy, commercial readiness, and progressing pipeline assets. Sinha added, “we expect our cash to be sufficient to fund operations into 2027.”

Outlook

  • Scholar Rock anticipates resubmitting the BLA and launching apitegromab in the U.S. in 2026, contingent on FDA reinspection results. The company expects to submit an sBLA for the second fill/finish facility later in 2026 and continues to expect a decision from the EMA on apitegromab near the middle of next year.
  • No explicit revenue or EPS guidance figures were disclosed in the transcript.

Financial Results

  • CFO Vikas Sinha stated operating expenses for the quarter were $103 million, including $18.3 million in noncash stock-based compensation. Excluding this, operating expenses were $85.3 million, reflecting investments in infrastructure for regulatory approval, commercial readiness, and clinical pipeline support.
  • The company added $141.7 million to its balance sheet in the quarter, with $91.7 million from an ATM share sale and $50 million from a debt facility drawdown. Sinha indicated that approximately $60 million in cash from outstanding warrant exercises is expected by year-end.

Q&A

  • Mani Foroohar, Leerink Partners: Asked about financing strategy and operational risks in transitioning manufacturing facilities. CFO Sinha explained prioritizing non-dilutive financing and minimizing use of ATM, while Hallal stressed efforts for a seamless supply chain transition with minimal operational risk.
  • Eric Schmidt, Cantor Fitzgerald: Queried confidence in remediation at the manufacturing facility. Hallal highlighted Novo Nordisk’s progress and commitment to quality, while Vaishnaw affirmed the FDA’s urgency and collaborative approach.
  • Tessa Romero, JPMorgan: Sought clarity on the BLA resubmission and EMA review. Hallal confirmed the plan to resubmit with Catalent as primary filler, with a second filler added post-approval. Vaishnaw noted the EMA review is progressing as expected, with mutual recognition of quality standards.
  • Tazeen Ahmad, BofA Securities: Asked about reinspection timing and contingency if issues persist. Hallal described the importance of successful reinspection and outlined the accelerated efforts on the second vialer as a contingency.
  • Kripa Devarakonda, Truist Securities: Inquired about resubmission timing and Novo Nordisk’s use of consultants. Vaishnaw confirmed resubmission would follow reinspection, with readiness to file quickly, and Hallal said Novo Nordisk has involved outside experts.
  • Etzer Darout, Barclays: Questioned the regulatory process post-resubmission and timelines. Vaishnaw stated they await FDA meeting minutes and will be guided by the agency on review timelines.
  • Marc Frahm, TD Cowen: Asked about disclosure practices around inspection timelines and potential for resubmission to prompt inspection. Hallal committed to open disclosure of material information, while Vaishnaw emphasized collaboration with the FDA rather than forcing function.
  • Evan Seigerman, BMO: Asked about market research and patient identification ahead of launch. Woods detailed increased engagement with physician teams and advocacy groups, building readiness for launch.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on regulatory and manufacturing risks, expressing cautious optimism but pressing for clarity on timelines and contingency plans. Their tone was neutral to slightly negative, with persistent questions around operational risk and regulatory delays.
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone in prepared remarks and Q&A, frequently emphasizing collaboration, urgency, and readiness. CEO Hallal repeatedly stated confidence in eventual approval, using phrases like “we know that it is not a matter of if but when apitegromab will be approved.”
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts’ questions were more risk-focused, while management shifted from an anticipatory stance on approval to addressing regulatory setbacks and mitigation strategies.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance shifted from expectations of imminent approval and launch in Q3 2025 to a focus on overcoming regulatory delays and targeting a 2026 U.S. launch.
  • Strategic focus moved from general commercial readiness and global rollout to supply chain redundancy and regulatory compliance.
  • Analysts’ attention shifted from label and payer discussions in the previous quarter to operational and regulatory risk in the current quarter.
  • Key financial metrics evolved, with the company reporting increased cash on hand and deferred operational investments compared to the prior quarter.
  • Management’s confidence transitioned from preparing for launch to navigating and mitigating unexpected regulatory obstacles, while still projecting long-term optimism about prospects.

Risks and Concerns

  • The primary challenge remains FDA approval of apitegromab, delayed by compliance issues at the third-party fill/finish facility. Scholar Rock is mitigating this risk by accelerating a second supply facility.
  • Regulatory unpredictability and inspection outcomes represent ongoing risks, as highlighted by multiple analyst questions.
  • Management is deferring some investments and hiring until approval is secured, preserving financial flexibility while prioritizing key initiatives.

Final Takeaway

Scholar Rock’s leadership emphasized that the regulatory setback for apitegromab is temporary, with the company aiming for resubmission and potential U.S. launch in 2026. The management team is accelerating supply chain redundancy and maintaining financial discipline to support pipeline progress, while reiterating confidence in the long-term opportunity for apitegromab and the broader neuromuscular disease portfolio.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Nucor, Cleveland-Cliffs upgraded at Wells Fargo on higher sheet steel price forecast
data.description:
Nucor (NUE) +2.3% and Cleveland-Cliffs (CLF) +1.3% in Friday's trading as Wells Fargo upgraded the steel producers to Overweight and Equal Weight, respectively, as the bank raised its U.S. steel price forecast with more evidence of its price squeeze thesis playing out, as sharply lower imports and lower inventory prompt greater mill pricing power into H1 2026.

The bank lowered its Q4 U.S. hot rolled coil benchmark forecast to $850/st from $880/st, but raised its estimate for 2026 to $875/st from a prior $850/st. 

Wells Fargo analyst Timna Tanners said Nucor's (NUE) Overweight rating and $167 price target, raised from $147, reflects the higher sheet steel forecasts for 2026, capex that should drop by $500M, and recent years outsized spending that should fade into 2027; as such, free cash flow should improve in 2027, and the company can benefit from growing data center related demand.

Tanners said her Equal Weight rating and $12 PT for Cleveland-Cliffs (CLF) reflects the near-term constructive view on sheet steel prices but concern over longer-term competitiveness, noting the company's contracts and Canadian presence can limit upside; a sharp upswing in estimated FY 2026 EBITDA is justified but already somewhat anticipated, the analyst said.

data.title: MGM tops list of most shorted S&P 500 consumer discretionary stocks in October; Amazon remains least shorted
data.description:
Average short interest across the S&P 500 consumer discretionary stocks was 2.39% at the end of October, MGM Resorts International (MGM) being the most shorted stock in the sector and Amazon.com (AMZN) as the least shorted in the sector.

Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLY) has been up 2.42% so far this year, as compared to the over 14.55% rise in the broader S&P 500 index over the same period.

Stocks with the largest and least short positions

Ranked by short interest as a percentage of shares float

MGM Resorts International (NASDAQ:MGM) was the most shorted stock at 11.19% of shares float, followed by Pool Corp. (NASDAQ:POOL) and Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. (NCLH) with short interest of 9.76% and 7.62%, respectively.

Bets against Broadline Retail, represented by Amazon.com (NASDAQ:AMZN) remained the least shorted stock, with 70.08M shares sold short, or 0.73% of the shares float, followed by McDonald's (NYSE:MCD) and The Home Depot (NYSE:HD) with short interest of 1.02% and 1.04%, respectively.

Industry Analysis

Average short interest as a percentage of floating shares

 

Computer and Electronics Retail remained the most shorted industry within the sector, though short interest eased to 6.28% in October from 7.64% at the end of September.

Apparel, Accessories and Luxury Goods stood at 6.21%, down from 6.34% last month, followed by Home Furnishing ranking next at 5.64%, up from 5.02% in the end of September.

Overall, short positioning across the consumer discretionary sector remained relatively steady this month, with only minor shifts observed across most sub-industries.
